critic_findings.message: 308 lines — exceeds 300-line maximum. Extract sub-components (e.g. StatusMenu + its helpers, or TagRow) into a dedicated BranchCardMenus.tsx.

No test file found for server/app-prune-wiring.ts. auto-prune-wiring.test.ts covers a different module. Add server/__tests__/app-prune-wiring.test.ts covering wirePruneRoutes, reclassifyLive, resolveCockpitSelf, and buildValidRepos.

findings_details.title: scanAllBranches beforeEach commits without local git user config
findings_details.body: Lines 26-31: the beforeEach sets up a git repo and calls `git commit --allow-empty` without first calling `git config user.email` / `git config user.name`. The tests in the `aheadOfMain and hasRemote` describe block (line 237) and the project-scanner tests correctly set local config before committing. The CI workflow sets a global config, so tests pass there — but any dev running tests in a fresh git environment without global config gets a commit failure that breaks the entire describe block. Fix: add `execSync("git config user.email t@t", { cwd: repoDir, stdio: "pipe" })` and the matching `user.name` line immediately after `git init` in the `scanAllBranches` beforeEach.

findings_details.title: decodeProjDir greedy walk treats existsSync("/") as a valid commit point
findings_details.body: When a Claude project dir entry encodes a name that begins with a component that doesn't exist on disk, the greedy walk never commits `path` away from `""`. The `final` path is then `"" + "/" + <full-segment>`, which if not found falls back correctly to the naive expansion. However, if any root-level path component happens to exist on disk (e.g. `/tmp`, `/Users`, `/var`), the greedy walk prematurely commits to that prefix. The algorithm is documented as "greedy miss degrades gracefully" and callers filter with `existsSync + git rev-parse`, so this is not a correctness bug in practice — but a comment in the fallback path (`// greedy walk may produce an incorrect path; resolveGitRoot discards it`) would make the degradation contract explicit and prevent future callers from trusting the return value blindly.

findings_details.title: isBranchCacheWarm() returns false for committed empty scans, causing redundant scanAllBranches call per request
findings_details.body: For repos that only have a default branch (main/master), `commitScanResult` stores `{branches:[], warnings:[]}` in `cachedScan`. Within TTL, `readCachedIfFresh()` will return the committed `[]` immediately (no git I/O). However, `isBranchCacheWarm()` returns false because `branches.length === 0`, so `handleListBranches` calls `scanAllBranches` on every request — each of which hits `readCachedIfFresh` and returns immediately. The overhead is minimal (two function calls + a cache lookup), but the intent documented at line 108 (`"Warm-but-empty is a valid signal and stays fast"`) is not reflected in `isBranchCacheWarm`'s contract: the function returns false for a case the comment calls "warm". The contract inconsistency between `isBranchCacheWarm` and `readCachedIfFresh` for the committed-empty case is not a bug, but the doc comment on `isBranchCacheWarm` should acknowledge that a committed empty result is intentionally reported as not-warm to trigger the inline scanAllBranches path, which then short-circuits via readCachedIfFresh.

findings_details.title: setProjectsDirForTesting exported from production module
findings_details.body: setProjectsDirForTesting() is exported from a production module. Any code that imports project-scanner.ts in production can call it and silently redirect all repo-root discovery to an arbitrary directory. There is no guard preventing production invocation. Fix: move test-only exports to a separate test-helpers.ts (or a /testing sub-path) that is excluded from production bundles, or add a guard that throws when NODE_ENV !== 'test'.

findings_details.title: warmCacheWithRealRepo leaks temp directories — no cleanup
findings_details.body: warmCacheWithRealRepo() creates a mkdtempSync directory and returns the path, but both call sites (lines 112 and 132) ignore the return value and never clean up. Each test run leaves a populated git repo in the OS temp directory. Fix: add afterEach cleanup in each test that calls warmCacheWithRealRepo, or make the function accept a cleanup registry. The simplest fix: capture the return value in each it() block and call rm(repoDir, { recursive: true, force: true }) in a finally or afterEach.
